results.name: decepticon
results.description: One of the primary factions in the Transformers mythos. They are typically concerned with such things as conquering Cybertron, defeating the Autobots, amassing large quantities of energon, developing powerful weaponry, and beating people up. Not necessarily in that order.

results.name: deck of many things
results.description: The legendary deck and its cards.

results.name: deck requirement
results.description: Cards that require you to have a deck with specific attributes, or with an effect that requires you to have your deck be built with specific attributes.

results.name: deerstalker
results.description: The classic detective hat

results.name: defector
results.description: Permanents that move around the board on their own

results.name: deformed avacyn's collar
results.description: The (warped) symbol of her (corrupted) church.

results.name: dekella
results.description: Thassa's bident. Well, Kiora's now.

results.name: delayed cantrip
results.description: Draw a card next turn.

results.name: delayed payment
results.description: Cards which you can play now, and pay for later

results.name: delayed trigger
results.description: Create a triggered ability that may trigger later. Similar to a [reflexive trigger](reflexive-trigger).

results.name: delve
results.description: Exile cards from your graveyard to reduce the cost

results.name: demilich effect
results.description: Exiles cards from graveyard, but lets you cast copies of them. Essentially (usually) one-time recursion

results.name: deprecated card types
results.description: Cards featuring deprecated card types in some of their prints.

results.name: deprecated legend restriction
results.description: Until Champions of Kamigawa introduced the Legendary Supertype, the Legend rule was built into the creature type, meaning that certain cards would bring it up in the rules for their abilities.

results.name: deprecated legend type
results.description: These cards were originally printed with the Legend creature type. Their type line read “Summon Legend” or “Creature — Legend”.

results.name: deprecated mechanics
results.description: Mechanics that have been deprecated by newer developments.

results.name: deprecated p/t counter
results.description: In the early days Magic used all kinds of power/toughness modifying counters. Nowadays it just sticks with +1/+1 and -1/-1 counters.

results.name: deprecated untapped artifact
results.description: Until 6th edition, artifacts were deactivated when they were tapped. These cards from prior to 6ed have that behaviour baked into their current rules text.

results.name: deprecated wall restriction
results.description: Until Champions of Kamigawa introduced defender, the Wall subtype would make a creature unable to attack. As such, certain cards used to exempt Walls for balance reasons.

results.name: desolation (planet)
results.description: Setting for the Doctor Who episode "The Ghost Monument"

results.name: detain
results.description: Prevent a creature from attacking, blocking, or using it's activated abilities until one's next turn.

results.name: devour
results.description: Before a creature enters the battlefield, the player may sacrifice a number of permanents and the creature will enter with +1/+1 counters on it in relation to the number of permanents sacrificed.

results.name: dexterity
results.description: Cards that require physical interaction outside of normal game scope, such as tossing cards or specific usage of one's head or hands. This can be an accessibility issue for some who have limited mobility, so it's now only used as an un-set mechanic.

results.name: diana
results.description: Character from the Dungeons and Dragons cartoon series

results.name: dice roll
results.description: Cards that make you roll dice. For things that like or help when you roll dice, see [Synergy-Dice](synergy-dice)

results.name: diegetic artwork
results.description: The card illustration is in-universe artwork. Characters would recognize it as a work of art.
